D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Warning Colour Standards — Red, Yellow, Orange

ANSI Z535 and ISO 3864 specify warning colour: red = danger, orange = warning, yellow = caution; bright contrast and outline shape encode hazard severity

Standard hazard colours: red = danger (fatal if violated), orange = warning (serious injury), yellow = caution (minor injury), green = safe / first-aid, blue = mandatory information. Shape conventions: triangle = warning, circle with bar = prohibition, square = mandatory.

Pick severity tier.
